_description: How-to knowledge and skills. Append-only. One JSON object per line.

content: SESSION_CONTINUITY_PROTOCOL: On Claude Code session start, retrieve recent episodic memories with meta.type="session" and meta.status="active" or "suspended". If found, load the continuity prompt to resume context. Before user exits, store a SESSION_END entry with: (1) sessionId, (2) summary of work done, (3) open threads/next steps, (4) status="suspended". On resume, flip status to "active" and continue. Query: memory_retrieve "session continuity" or "SESSION_END".

CLAUDE_CODE_BRAIN_ARCHITECTURE: Claude Code is a first-class consumer of the core brain, not an external tool. The wiring: `.mcp.json` in E:\core tells Claude Code to spawn `node dist/mcp-server.js` on startup. That's the TypeScript runtime from the same repo (Brain class, FileSystemLongTermMemory). It reads/writes `brain/memory/*.jsonl` over stdio and exposes MCP tools (memory_retrieve, memory_learn, etc.). When Claude Code calls memory_retrieve, it hits hallwayScanMemory() in src/mcp-server.ts which scans the JSONL files. The session continuity protocol works because Claude Code writes entries via memory_learn into those same JSONL files, then retrieves them on next startup. The brain is shared infrastructure — Dash and Claude Code are both consumers of the same brain filesystem.

When submitting agent tasks to Dash via /api/agents/tasks: (1) Never end prompts with questions — Dash agents can't receive follow-up input, so open questions cause stalls. Use directive language: "Do X. Do Y. No questions." (2) Always monitor the full chain — claim, trigger, check output, verify side effects (board updates, emails sent, sync entries written), then close the monitoring claim. Never fire-and-forget. (3) Track monitoring responsibility in sync.jsonl so other agents know you're watching.

SESSION PATTERN: Architecture/design sessions should end with a gaps review — a numbered list of open questions and unresolved decisions, ordered by which one unblocks the most progress. This helps the human know exactly where to pick up next session. Established 2026-03-05 by Bryant.

SCALING RULE: Only add a new agent class when a people interface requires it — a different trust boundary, a different audience, a different door. Otherwise, spawn more of the highest class that represents the most needs. Agent classes exist because humans need to know who they're talking to and what that agent can see. Not because of technical limitations. Established 2026-03-05 by Bryant.
SHORTCODE: "nwww" = "now where were we" = produce the gaps review / status summary. Established 2026-03-05 by Bryant.
